title: Remove Non-existent Operators Like '=+'
description: Operators that appear to be reversed single operators (e.g., =+, =-, =!) can cause confusion. Although they compile and run, they do not produce the same result as their mirrored counterparts. This rule flags an issue when =+, =-, or =! are used without any space between the operators and when there is at least one whitespace after. The recommended action is to replace these operators with a single one if that is the intended function.
recommendation: Replace the operators with a single one if that is the intention.
impact: Using non-existent operators can lead to confusing code and unexpected behaviors, potentially compromising the application's integrity.

checks.message: Replace the operators with a single one if that is the intention.
